title: Tarasa joergensenii

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Peru, for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Tarasa joergensenii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly consumed in the form of capsules or tablets, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential applications.
